calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-17-2024 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given, and there being no appearance or objection filed, the court ordered the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY (CONTINUED)
Off Calendar May-15-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Sep-17-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-14-2024 Continued To May-15-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. IRVING PEDROZA, Esq. of D'Egiddio & Pedroza, APC (tel: 619-550-3011) for Plaintiff.
Atty. RICHARD LINKERT, Esq. of Matheny Sears Linkert & Jaime LLP (tel: 916-978-3434) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL JOHNSON's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Amended Complaint.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Plaintiff's counsel failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. The Court allows oral argument to be presented. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion to file amended complaint is denied.

Plaintiff filed his case in 2021. It is set for trial in five weeks, May 13, 2024. This would give defendant one week to conduct discovery of plaintiff's new theories, file a dispositive motion and retain an expert. That is unreasonable and prejudicial. (See Solit v. Tokai Bank (1999) 68 Cal.App.4th 1435, 1448.) Trial continuances in this court are granted only by the presiding judge in Department 206.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Mary Gallagher, CSR# 10749 (tel: 530-409-2629/ e-mail: mgcourtreporting@comcast.net)
